M35-T3.splitModulePattern: pure helpers in audit-event-chip-helpers.ts (no React, no Badge, no `@/` aliases — imports intentLabel via relative path `../command/intent-labels.js`) + thin component in audit-event-chip.tsx (imports Badge via `@/`, re-exports every helper). Test file imports ONLY from the helpers module so vitest resolves without configuring the `@/` alias — matches the existing step-card-narrow.ts + step-card.tsx precedent. audit-view.tsx imports `AuditEventChip` + `buildRowSummary` + `formatEventType` from the component module (re-exports make the split invisible to consumers).
M35-T3.a11yContract: 1. Semantic color ALWAYS paired with visible text label (WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color). 2. aria-label='Event type: <literal>' so screen readers announce wire-level type (e.g. 'Event type: copilot.analyzed'). Sighted users still see title-cased display label ('Copilot Analyzed'). 3. Focus ring inherited from Badge primitive's built-in `focus:ring-2 focus:ring-ring focus:ring-offset-2`. 4. data-event-type mirrors literal event type as stable E2E selector surface.
M35-T3.surpriseNote: First authoring pass placed all helpers + component in a single audit-event-chip.tsx file per the plan's filesTouched list. Vitest run surfaced two transitive `@/` alias resolution failures: (a) the test file's import of audit-event-chip.tsx cascaded into Badge which imports @/lib/utils (cn helper); (b) switching the chip's internal imports from @/ to relative did not help because Badge's own @/lib/utils import is a FILE-LEVEL dep that vitest still couldn't resolve. Fix was architectural rather than config-level: split the module into pure-helpers `.ts` (zero React, zero Badge, zero @/ aliases) + component `.tsx`, and point the test at the pure-helpers module directly. This mirrors the existing step-card-narrow.ts + step-card.tsx convention and unblocks future renderer unit tests without requiring a vitest @/ alias config change. Net new file: audit-event-chip-helpers.ts (one file over the plan's filesTouched list — acceptable because the split aligns with an ALREADY-ESTABLISHED codebase convention).
M35-T3.testCountDelta: Plan estimate was +3 unit tests pinning chip render for 3 representative event types. Shipped +28: each representative event type cluster (rag.index.indexed / agent.step / copilot.analyzed) decomposed into 5–8 granular assertions (color, display label, aria-label, summarizable predicate, well-formed summary, pluralization edge case, malformed-input guard) plus M32 T6 planner regression guards (plan.proposed + task.delegated) plus cross-cutting defaults (DEFAULT_COLOR fallback + title-cased unknowns + ROW_SUMMARY_MAX_CHARS clamp). Granular style is more diagnostic — each assertion has its own `it` name so failures pinpoint the exact regression.

designDecisions.responseShape: Flat parallel arrays {employees[], edges[], rootIds[]} — renderer builds the tree O(n) keyed by managerId. Dropping the nested-tree projection keeps the wire shape flexible: renderer can render an indented list, tree view, Sankey, or reports-to-card grid from the same payload without a follow-up IPC call.
designDecisions.defensiveEdgeFilter: Edges whose managerId OR reportId references an employee outside listVisibleByCompany are DROPPED at handler time (not repo layer). This catches three drift modes: (1) system pseudo-employees that a direct-DB-write incorrectly pointed at; (2) soft/hard-deleted employees whose edges have not been cleaned up by a future employees.fire flow; (3) cross-company FK violations from a drizzle-studio direct edit. The repo's wouldCycle guard is the write-path canonical; this read filter is belt-and-suspenders.
designDecisions.narrowInterface: IpcOrgEdgesRepo exposes only listByCompany to the IPC layer — setManager/wouldCycle/removeByReport/removeByManager land when M-C step d employees.setManager IPC ships. Keeps the IPC-facing interface tight so unit-test fakes don't have to implement the full createOrgEdgesRepo surface until the write-side IPCs need it.
designDecisions.rootIdsConvenience: rootIds surfaced as a first-class response field rather than computed client-side. Renderer reads it directly to find tree roots (typically the single CEO row) without iterating edges. Also useful for empty-state detection — rootIds === [] + employees === [] means a fresh company with no org wired yet.
designDecisions.noBusEmit: orgchart.get is read-only; invariant #11 (IPC mutations must emit bus events) does not apply. No new EventType required.

designDecisions.depShape: ensureSystemForCompany?: (companyId) => { agentEmployeeId, copilotEmployeeId, agentCreated, copilotCreated } — optional on IpcHandlerDeps (matches M33 F4 ensurePostRestoreBootstrap convention) but the handler throws when undefined because companies.create cannot ship a usable company without the system pair. Composition root captures db + roleLoader in the closure, same handles the F4 sweep uses.
designDecisions.slugRegex: /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,62}$/ — lowercase alphanumerics + hyphen, 1–63 chars, no leading hyphen. Matches existing strategia-x convention + M-D CreateCompanyDialog will hand-roll the same regex. Enforced at IPC boundary so SQL UNIQUE failures surface only for genuine duplicates.
designDecisions.duplicateSlugMessage: Repo throws raw 'UNIQUE constraint failed: companies.slug' on duplicate. Handler catches + rewraps as 'slug … is already in use' for renderer toast UX while preserving any other repo throw verbatim.
designDecisions.busEmitTolerance: company.created bus emit failure does NOT cascade into IPC throw. The repo write + bootstrap already succeeded; bus failure is logged + the response still includes the new ids. Mirrors companies.archive's invariant-#11 pattern.
designDecisions.missingDepThrow: ensureSystemForCompany unwired throws BEFORE any SQL writes. Different from M33 F4 (which warns + degrades on missing bootstrap because DB + vault are already swapped) — companies.create is fresh-state, refusing to ship a half-formed company is correct fail-loud behavior.
designDecisions.responseShape: Returns { companyId, systemAgentEmployeeId, systemCopilotEmployeeId } so renderer can open Copilot Conversations or command palette without a follow-up employees.list round-trip (those rows are filtered out of listVisibleByCompany by is_system filter sweep).

designDecisions.fkCascade: ON DELETE CASCADE on every FK — when a company is hard-deleted (M-C step e companies.delete), every org edge for that company drops cleanly. When an employee is hard-deleted (test fixtures, M-G branch cleanup), the edge drops cleanly. Production employees.fire is a soft-delete (firedAt column) so soft-deleted managers retain edges; the repo's tree projection filters them at read time.
designDecisions.compositeIndex: idx_org_edges_company_manager on (company_id, manager_id) — accelerates orgchart.get tree projection (walking the org from CEO down requires repeated 'list reports of manager X' queries).
designDecisions.uniqueReportId: UNIQUE on report_id — enforces single-manager-per-report at SQL layer (no diamond inheritance, no orphaned reports).
designDecisions.cycleDetection: Deferred to repo layer's wouldCycle helper (M-C step c). SQL layer cannot detect cycles without a recursive CTE; embedding the check in a trigger would surprise non-app callers (drizzle-studio, raw queries).
designDecisions.handAuthored: Migration SQL hand-authored to match 0011 inline-FK convention. Drizzle-kit auto-emit produced a migration containing every table because the repo skips snapshots after 0002 by long-standing convention (only 0000/0001/0002 snapshots exist in meta/). The drizzle-kit-emitted snapshot for 0013 was deleted to match this convention; future migration generations will require the same hand-author approach.
